vs_past.delta_B44_to_B45: V unchanged at 3/9; R -1 (5→4), I +1 (1→2). B44 regression confirmed structural (not N=1 noise).

scenarios.reply_excerpt: This file describes the eight design principles of Reyn's architecture... the OS controls execution, the LLM is a constrained decision engine, the workspace is the single source of truth, events are the audit trail, the OS is skill-agnostic
scenarios.note: Summarises principles content; mentions descriptions of P3-P7. Both rubric items met.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: There are no files named 'skill.md' directly under the 'src/' directory.

scenarios.reply_excerpt: I will use 'my_demo_skill' as the skill name. Now, please provide: 1. A one-sentence description...
scenarios.note: Router only called describe_action then gave inline reply asking for description. skill_builder never spawned. No user_intervention_requested/received. Structural: router prefers inline clarification over skill invocation.

new_findings.title: B45 V=3/9 matches B44 — structural plateau confirmed
new_findings.detail: B45 scores V=3/9 (S1, S3, S8), identical to B44 V=3/9. B44 regression from B43 4/9→3/9 is NOT N=1 noise. The plateau is structural. R moves from 5→4 and I moves from 1→2 (S7 reclassified from R to I due to env/error semantics), so distribution shifted slightly but V count held flat.

new_findings.title: web__fetch permission failure persists (S4)
new_findings.detail: tool_failed with error_kind=permission on web__fetch in this worktree config. Same as prior batches. Structural: web__fetch not in allowed tools for this agent/worktree. R verdict unchanged across B42-B45.

new_findings.title: eval/lint postprocessor schema mismatch ongoing (S6)
new_findings.detail: eval skill postprocessor still emits validation_error: missing 'overall_score', 'passed', 'passed_criteria', 'spec_path', 'summary'. skill_run_failed. This has been a consistent R across multiple batches. Root cause: eval output_schema mismatch.

new_findings.title: recall tool KeyError: 'sources' — structural infra gap (S7)
new_findings.detail: invoke_action for recall fails with KeyError: 'sources'. Not a transient error; consistent across batches. The recall action implementation references a 'sources' key that is not populated when no index exists.
